metadata.title: The Model Isn't Broken — It's Honest About What It Learned
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Computational Skepticism for AI candidate 17. Source: books/computational-skepticism-for-ai/chapters/04-robustness-what-understanding-means-when-a-pixel-can-break-the-model.md (adversarial examples and proxy features, lines 11–65).
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = correct diagnosis / proxy-learner framing / representation interrogation; CRIMSON = wrong diagnosis / fragile framing / patching; SLATE = the panda / shape channel / what humans see; GOLD = the flip moment / adversarial perturbation / the reveal
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: no Ilyas et al. experimental details, no robust-dataset debate, no defense toolkit, no gradient math
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B15: palette=claude but the outro is 'OutroCTA' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. The model isn't broken — it's honest about what it learned.

card.copy:
The Model Isn't Broken —
It's Honest About What It Learned

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. The model isn't broken — it's honest about what it learned.

beats.narration_text: An image classifier looks at a panda and reports 'panda' with 94% confidence. You add an invisible perturbation — noise imperceptible to the human eye. The classifier now reports 'gibbon' with 99.3% confidence. The panda is still there. It didn't move.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: An image classifier looks at a panda and reports 'panda' with 94% confidence. You add an invisible perturbation — noise imperceptible to the human eye. The clas

beats.narration_text: If the image looks identical to human eyes, what exactly changed — and what does the flip tell you about what the model was actually looking at?

props.headline: If the image looks identical to human eyes, what exactly changed…

card.copy: If the image looks identical, what changed — and what does the flip reveal about what the model was actually looking at?

beats.narration_text: The natural reaction is: the model is brittle. Patch it. Add more training data. Smooth the outputs. That reaction misses what actually happened. The model isn't fragile. It learned the wrong thing — and the perturbation reveals the difference.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The natural reaction is: the model is brittle. Patch it. Add more training data. Smooth the outputs. That reaction misses what actually happened. The model isn'

beats.narration_text: The panda image has two components. A shape channel — the outlines, pose, and color that humans recognize as 'panda.' And a texture channel — high-frequency statistical patterns invisible to humans. The perturbation left the shape channel unchanged and slid the texture channel from panda-signature to gibbon-signature.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The panda image has two components. A shape channel — the outlines, pose, and color that humans recognize as 'panda.' And a texture channel — high-frequency sta

beats.narration_text: The model was never looking at what you thought it was looking at.

props.headline: The model was never looking at what you thought it was…

beats.image_description: Close-up of digital pixel noise or static pattern in black and white, abstract and grainy, suggesting hidden structure in what looks random

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The model was never looking at what you thought it was looking at.

beats.narration_text: The model has learned what we call a proxy — a feature in the input distribution that correlated reliably with the correct label on the training data. The engineers thought the model learned what pandas look like. The model learned the statistical signature of pixel arrangements that occurred in panda training images. On the training set these are indistinguishable. Under adversarial perturbation they fly apart.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The model has learned what we call a proxy — a feature in the input distribution that correlated reliably with the correct label on the training data. The engin

beats.narration_text: The model has learned a proxy — a feature in the input distribution that correlated reliably with the right label on the training data. The engineers thought the model had learned what pandas look like. The model had actually learned the statistical signature of pixel arrangements that occurred in panda training images.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The model has learned a proxy — a feature in the input distribution that correlated reliably with the right label on the training data. The engineers thought th

beats.narration_text: If you diagnose this as fragility, you patch it — harden it against this perturbation, train it on noisy inputs. A slightly different perturbation finds a new proxy. The attack surface moves, but the underlying problem doesn't. If you diagnose it correctly, you interrogate the representation — ask what the model is actually betting on.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: If you diagnose this as fragility, you patch it — harden it against this perturbation, train it on noisy inputs. A slightly different perturbation finds a new p

beats.narration_text: Adversarial perturbations are not bugs, they are features — genuinely predictive, statistically real patterns that happen to be imperceptible and meaningless to humans. They reveal what the model actually responds to. They tell you which features the model has bet on.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Adversarial perturbations are not bugs, they are features — genuinely predictive, statistically real patterns that happen to be imperceptible and meaningless to

beats.narration_text: A 94% accuracy benchmark doesn't tell you the model is robust, or that it learned what you intended it to learn. It tells you the features the model bet on happened to win on the training distribution. The perturbation is the audit.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A 94% accuracy benchmark doesn't tell you the model is robust, or that it learned what you intended it to learn. It tells you the features the model bet on happ

beats.narration_text: A skin lesion classifier scores ninety-two percent on the test set. A researcher examines the high-confidence predictions and notices: nearly every malignant case in the training data includes a ruler in the image. Dermatologists photographed suspicious lesions with measurement tools. The model learned 'ruler means cancer.' When tested on images without rulers, accuracy drops to sixty-two percent. The model wasn't wrong — it was honest about what it had learned.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A skin lesion classifier scores ninety-two percent on the test set. A researcher examines the high-confidence predictions and notices: nearly every malignant ca

beats.narration_text: The panda never moved. What the model was actually using to classify it did. Adversarial examples aren't a defect — they're a diagnostic.

props.headline: The panda never moved. What the model was actually using to…

card.copy: The panda never moved. What the model was looking at did.
card.sub: Adversarial examples aren't a defect — they're a diagnostic.
